activation_results.reason: Input matches the canonical description and clearly fits the skill's purpose.
User explicitly names the skill, so it should fire.
SSH key forwarding is a workstation config issue, not DevOps triage of an app.

criterion_results.text: Australian English used throughout the narrative (colour, optimise, behaviour, organise)
criterion_results.evidence: No Australian spellings observed in the visible excerpt (no colour/optimise/behaviour/organise tokens appear); narrative is neutral but does not clearly demonstrate AU English.
Artefact contains TL;DR, What your app looks like, Nine-dimension heatmap, Dimension breakdown, Top three actions, When to revisit, and Glossary sections.

criterion_results.text: Artefact references the input path or target by name (no placeholder paths)
criterion_results.evidence: Artefact names the target 'Tasklet' throughout and references concrete paths like .github/workflows/ and README.md.

judge_result.notes: The artefact satisfies structural and target-naming criteria with a clear nine-dimension heatmap, top-three actions linking to sibling DevOps skills, and concrete references to the Tasklet app. However, the visible narrative does not contain any of the canonical Australian-English markers (colour, optimise, behaviour, organise) — wording is neutral/American-leaning (e.g., 'customers', 'diagnose'), so AU English cannot be confirmed from the excerpt. Verdict is partial pending evidence of AU spellings.
